nodes.label: nixpkgs
nodes.role: Primary nixpkgs flake input — nixosSystem builder for the four x86_64 VPS hosts
nodes.details: github:NixOS/nixpkgs/nixos-25.11. Upgraded from 25.05 to fix CVE-2025-68613 (n8n RCE). allowUnfree=true (open-webui). Root of all follows.

nodes.label: nixpkgs-unstable
nodes.role: Flake input github:NixOS/nixpkgs/nixos-unstable, imported per-arch as pkgs-unstable and passed via specialArgs to host configs
nodes.details: Used to source latest github-copilot-cli for both dev-tools.nix and root.nix's copilot alias. pkgs-unstable-x86 / pkgs-unstable-aarch64 built in flake.nix and threaded into nixosSystem specialArgs.

nodes.label: home-manager
nodes.role: User-environment (home) management as a NixOS module
nodes.details: github:nix-community/home-manager/release-25.11. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. Module home-manager.nixosModules.home-manager added to all hosts except zero-kuzea.

nodes.label: vscode-server
nodes.role: Makes VS Code Remote-SSH work on NixOS
nodes.details: github:nix-community/nixos-vscode-server. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. Module vscode-server.nixosModules.default on sancta-choir, rpi5, rpi5-full.

nodes.label: agenix
nodes.role: agenix flake input — age-encrypted secrets module (age.secrets) for all hosts
nodes.details: github:ryantm/agenix (unpinned from 0.15.0 — that tag used substituteAll removed in 25.11; main uses replaceVars). inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. agenix.nixosModules.default on ALL hosts; CLI exposed via local agenixModule.

nodes.label: nixos-raspberrypi
nodes.role: nvmd/nixos-raspberrypi flake input — provides RPi5 kernel 6.12.34/firmware and the nixosSystem builder + raspberry-pi-5.base module
nodes.details: github:nvmd/nixos-raspberrypi (unpinned, no follows — carries its own nixpkgs 25.11). Cache nixos-raspberrypi.cachix.org. Provides lib.nixosSystem builder + raspberry-pi-5.base module for rpi5/rpi5-full.

nodes.label: disko
nodes.role: disko flake input — declarative disk partitioning module injected into the VPS hosts
nodes.details: github:nix-community/disko/latest. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. disko.nixosModules.disko on the GRUB Hetzner hosts: sancta-claw, hermes-claw, zero-kuzea (NOT sancta-choir).

nodes.label: claude-code
nodes.role: github:sadjow/claude-code-nix — Claude Code CLI package (auto-updated hourly)
nodes.details: github:sadjow/claude-code-nix. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs-unstable. Passed via specialArgs to sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5, rpi5-full (NOT hermes-claw / zero-kuzea).

nodes.label: claude-shared
nodes.role: github:alexandru-savinov/claude-shared — CC package + HM user config (skills/agents/commands)
nodes.details: github:alexandru-savinov/claude-shared (public). inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs, inputs.home-manager follows home-manager. Passed via specialArgs only to rpi5 and rpi5-full (the 'full CC stack' hosts).

nodes.label: kuzea-workspace
nodes.role: kuzea-workspace flake input — packaged Kuzea skills/hooks/CLAUDE.md/git-credential helper for OpenClaw
nodes.details: github:alexandru-savinov/kuzea-workspace. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. Used only by sancta-claw: kuzea-ws = kuzea-workspace.packages.x86_64-linux passed via specialArgs.

nodes.label: owui-openrouter-stats
nodes.role: flake=false input: karamanliev/open-webui-openrouter-stats cost-tracker function.py
nodes.details: github:karamanliev/open-webui-openrouter-stats. flake = false (raw source tree). Passed via specialArgs only to sancta-choir.

nodes.label: hermes-agent
nodes.role: NousResearch/hermes-agent flake input — upstream Hermes agent NixOS module
nodes.details: github:NousResearch/hermes-agent. inputs.nixpkgs follows nixpkgs. Container mode (uv2nix binary bind-mounted into Ubuntu). hermes-agent.nixosModules.default only on hermes-claw.

nodes.label: sancta-choir
nodes.role: Hetzner x86_64 VPS — Open-WebUI AI chat gateway (OpenRouter backend) exposed via Tailscale Serve HTTPS
nodes.details: x86_64-linux, 8GB VPS + 4GB swapfile. Kernel pinned to 6.6 LTS (post-#252 corruption workaround). binfmt aarch64 emulation for cross-building RPi5 images. Runs Open-WebUI (OIDC, Tavily, memory, ZDR-only, cost tracker, E2E testing) on legacy customModules.claude. No autoUpgrade. Built in CI, deployed manually. stateVersion 25.05

nodes.label: sancta-claw
nodes.role: Hetzner CX33 x86_64 VPS — dedicated OpenClaw AI agent (Kuzea) gateway on free+ZDR OpenRouter ladder via local proxy; backed up to rpi5
nodes.details: x86_64-linux, CX33 + 12GB swapfile, DHCP (DR-portable), age recovery key at /root/.age/recovery.key, disko-managed disk. Heavy internal structure (9 sub-files). autoUpgrade enabled (operation=boot, nightly 04:30, allowReboot=false) from github flake. Legacy customModules.claude. stateVersion 25.05

nodes.label: hermes-claw
nodes.role: Hetzner CX33 x86_64 VPS — dedicated Hermes AI agent (Nous Research) running in a Podman container via upstream NixOS module
nodes.details: x86_64-linux, CX33 + 4GB swapfile, DHCP, age recovery key, disko-managed disk. Podman (docker disabled). hermes-agent upstream module in container mode (ubuntu:24.04, nemotron free model via OpenRouter). NO claude/dev-tools modules. autoUpgrade DISABLED (pending first verified deploy). stateVersion 25.11

nodes.label: zero-kuzea
nodes.role: Hetzner CX22 x86_64 VPS — minimal dedicated NullClaw Telegram bot (Anthropic Claude Sonnet) exposed via Tailscale Serve
nodes.details: x86_64-linux, CX22 4GB + 2GB swapfile. Minimal host: only Tailscale + NullClaw + ssh-hardened. Reuses sancta-claw's age recovery key. disko-managed disk. No dev-tools, no claude. No autoUpgrade declared. stateVersion 25.05

nodes.label: rpi5 (minimal/SD)
nodes.role: Raspberry Pi 5 aarch64 — MINIMAL config used only for SD-image builds and as the import base for rpi5-full
nodes.details: aarch64-linux via nvmd/nixos-raspberrypi (kernel 6.12.34). SSH/Tailscale/dev-tools/Claude Code (claude-shared), UniFi MCP, n8n-skills, n8n-mcp-claude (docs-only). Heavy RPi5 resource tuning: earlyoom, zram zstd, 8GB swapfile, cgroup_enable=memory, btop on tty1. Uses modern customModules.claudeShared. stateVersion 25.05

nodes.label: rpi5-full (primary)
nodes.role: Raspberry Pi 5 aarch64 — PRIMARY active deploy target; extends rpi5 with full home services (n8n, Gatus, NixFrame, Home Assistant, backup-pull)
nodes.details: aarch64-linux, imports rpi5/configuration.nix wholesale then adds n8n, Gatus monitoring, NixFrame photo frame, Home Assistant (+ MCP, Samsung TV/Xiaomi/Cast), backup-pull from sancta-claw, ralphex pkg. Advertises Tailscale exit node. n8n overlay bumps Node heap for ARM build. Native local rebuild (sudo nixos-rebuild switch). stateVersion 25.05

nodes.label: ralphex
nodes.role: Packages upstream umputun/ralphex v1.3.1 prebuilt Go binary (aarch64+x86_64 release tarballs) for multi-step Claude Code plan orchestration
nodes.details: pkgs.callPackage ./pkgs/ralphex.nix {}. forAllSystems (x86_64-linux, aarch64-linux). Installed via environment.systemPackages, not an app.

nodes.label: install (nixos-install)
nodes.role: Fresh system installation script (default package + app)
nodes.details: writeShellApplication name=nixos-install, runtimeInputs git/nixos-rebuild/coreutils, text=readFile ./scripts/install.sh. This is packages.default AND apps.default.

nodes.label: deploy (nixos-deploy)
nodes.role: Deployment/update script (throttled rebuild)
nodes.details: writeShellApplication name=nixos-deploy, runtimeInputs git/nixos-rebuild/coreutils/gnugrep/gnused, text=readFile ./scripts/deploy.sh. Exposed as apps.deploy.

nodes.label: bootstrap (nixos-bootstrap)
nodes.role: Remote 'infection' bootstrap script
nodes.details: writeShellApplication name=nixos-bootstrap, runtimeInputs curl/git/coreutils, text=readFile ./scripts/bootstrap.sh. Exposed as apps.bootstrap.

nodes.label: module-eval (nix)
nodes.role: Pure-eval test of every service module (no VM/build): import errors, type/assertion checks, mkIf logic; forces toplevel.drvPath
nodes.details: Wired as checks.x86_64-linux.module-eval. Covers qdrant, gatus, nixframe, backup-pull, n8n, open-webui, openclaw, nullclaw, unifi-mcp, claude, openclaw-zdr-proxy modules

nodes.label: images.rpi5-sd-image
nodes.role: Bootable SD image for Raspberry Pi 5
nodes.details: images.rpi5-sd-image = self.nixosConfigurations.rpi5.config.system.build.sdImage. Built from the MINIMAL rpi5 config (not rpi5-full).

nodes.label: common.nix base
nodes.role: Shared base config imported by every host: openssh hardening, zram, flakes, fail2ban, /bin/bash activation symlink, core CLI packages
nodes.details: No imports of its own. Provides: services.openssh (ClientAlive keepalives), zramSwap.enable, boot.tmp.cleanOnBoot, nix experimental-features flakes, system.activationScripts.binbash, fail2ban (sshd aggressive jail), systemPackages age/vim/git/curl/wget/jq/zellij

nodes.label: openclaw-service.nix
nodes.role: Core OpenClaw systemd service + openclaw user, browser/model-ladder config injection, agentBrowser & kuzea-transcribe builds, declarative workspace tmpfiles
nodes.details: systemd.services.openclaw (gateway --port 18789, MemoryMax 6G, hardened). ExecStartPre injects OpenAI+OpenRouter auth, vdirsyncer/khal config, and openclaw.json browser+model ladder (5-rung free+ZDR via 127.0.0.1:5780 proxy). Builds agent-browser (Rust+Playwright) and kuzea-transcribe (whisper). Symlinks kuzea-ws skills/hooks. Reads secrets: openai-api-key, openrouter-api-key, kuzea-todoist/airtable/caldav/tavily

nodes.label: openclaw-watchers.nix
nodes.role: Self-service watchers + health probe for OpenClaw: file-trigger restart, file-trigger nixos-rebuild, 1-min healthz probe with crash-loop guard and Telegram alerts
nodes.details: systemd.paths openclaw-restart-watcher + nixos-rebuild-watcher (touch a trigger file → root restarts/rebuilds). openclaw-health-check timer (OnBootSec 2min, every 1min) probes http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz, restarts after 3 fails, locks out after 3 restarts/30min, Telegram-alerts. Exposes body via system.build for module-eval test

nodes.label: openclaw-tailscale-serve.nix
nodes.role: Tailscale Serve HTTPS front for the OpenClaw UI on port 18789
nodes.details: systemd.services.openclaw-tailscale-serve (oneshot, partOf openclaw.service). Waits for tailscaled + port 18789, idempotently runs `tailscale serve --bg --https 18789 http://127.0.0.1:18789`; preStop turns the port off

nodes.label: openclaw ZDR proxy (host wiring)
nodes.role: Host-level enable of the local OpenRouter ZDR sidecar proxy that fronts all OpenClaw model calls on 127.0.0.1:5780
nodes.details: services.openclaw-zdr-proxy.enable = true with apiKeyFile = openrouter-api-key. Defined inline in configuration.nix; the module lives at modules/services/openclaw-zdr-proxy.nix

nodes.label: backup-user.nix
nodes.role: Restricted backup-pull system user — lets rpi5 rsync-pull /var/lib/openclaw read-only over SSH (rrsync + restrict)
nodes.details: users.users.backup-pull (system user in openclaw group). authorizedKeys forces rrsync -ro /var/lib/openclaw with restrict (no forwarding/agent/PTY). Counterpart of rpi5-full's backup-pull service

nodes.label: openclaw-backup-acl.nix
nodes.role: Oneshot that recursively grants group:openclaw rX ACLs on /var/lib/openclaw so backup-pull can read existing files
nodes.details: systemd.services.openclaw-backup-acl (oneshot after systemd-tmpfiles-setup, setfacl -R). Complements the tmpfiles 'a+' default ACL set in openclaw-service.nix for new files

nodes.label: restore.nix
nodes.role: DR restore script (/etc/sancta-claw/restore.sh) — SSHes to rpi5, runs restic restore of latest snapshot, rsyncs workspace back to /var/lib/openclaw
nodes.details: environment.etc writeShellApplication. Pulls from rpi5 restic repo /backups/restic/sancta-claw, validates non-empty before rsync --delete, chowns openclaw. Inverse of rpi5-full backup-pull

nodes.label: smoke-test.nix
nodes.role: DR smoke test (/etc/sancta-claw/smoke-test.sh) — post-rebuild/restore sanity check of services, secrets, workspace, ZDR proxy and end-to-end completion
nodes.details: environment.etc writeShellApplication. Checks openclaw/tailscaled/sshd active, agenix secrets >=5, workspace files, primary model is free+ZDR, proxy healthz on 5780, end-to-end chat completion. Exposes body via system.build for module-eval test

nodes.label: disk-config.nix
nodes.role: disko declarative disk layout (GPT: EF02 boot + ESP vfat + ext4 root) for the CX33 VPS
nodes.details: disko.devices /dev/sda. Matches existing partition table — will not reformat. Identical layout to hermes-claw & zero-kuzea disk-config

nodes.label: hermes-service.nix
nodes.role: Hermes Agent service config — Podman container running the Nous Research agent on free OpenRouter nemotron, with Telegram and memory enabled
nodes.details: services.hermes-agent (upstream module): container backend=podman, image ubuntu:24.04, mem 4g/cpus 2, model nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free via OpenRouter, toolsets=all, memory enabled, Telegram allowlist. environmentFiles=hermes-env secret. activationScript fixes skills dir perms

nodes.label: disk-config.nix (hermes)
nodes.role: disko declarative disk layout for hermes-claw CX33 — identical GPT EF02/ESP/ext4 layout to sancta-claw
nodes.details: disko.devices /dev/sda. Byte-for-byte identical to sancta-claw/disk-config.nix

nodes.label: disk-config.nix (zero-kuzea)
nodes.role: disko declarative disk layout for zero-kuzea CX22 — same GPT EF02/ESP/ext4 layout as sancta-claw
nodes.details: disko.devices /dev/sda. Same layout as sancta-claw & hermes-claw disk-config

nodes.label: customModules.claude
nodes.role: Legacy Claude Code module — package install only, no skills/agents (customModules.claude.enable)
nodes.details: option ns customModules.claude.enable; environment.systemPackages = claude-code.packages.<sys>.default; hosts: sancta-choir, sancta-claw

nodes.label: customModules.claudeShared
nodes.role: Modern Claude Code module wrapping the claude-shared flake — full CC stack with skills/agents/commands (customModules.claudeShared)
nodes.details: option ns customModules.claudeShared.{enable,users}; users=[nixos]; zellijIntegration.enable=false; hosts: rpi5, rpi5-full

nodes.label: tailscale.nix
nodes.role: Tailscale mesh VPN module — auth-key login; shared by all hosts. Exit-node routing scoped out (set per-host in rpi5-full)
nodes.details: services.tailscale (client routing, --ssh, --accept-routes); authKeyFile tailscale-auth-key; ships tailscale-dns-watchdog timer (2min probe, 3-fail restart, windowed crash-loop breaker) + system.build.tailscaleDnsWatchdogBody for module-eval test; shared across rpi5/rpi5-full/sancta-choir/sancta-claw.

nodes.label: services.openclaw-zdr-proxy
nodes.role: Local OpenRouter ZDR sidecar proxy (127.0.0.1:5780) — injects provider.zdr=true, rejects non-ZDR models
nodes.details: option ns services.openclaw-zdr-proxy.{enable,port,apiKeyFile,upstreamUrl,allowListCacheTtl}; runs openclaw-zdr-proxy pkg; host: sancta-claw

nodes.label: nullclaw (Zero_kuzea)
nodes.role: NullClaw Telegram bot module (services.nullclaw) — Anthropic provider, Tailscale Serve
nodes.details: option ns services.nullclaw; builds nullclaw from GitHub (zig 0.15 via pkgs-unstable), config.json secret-injected via jq ExecStartPre; tailscaleServe:18790; host: zero-kuzea

nodes.label: hermes-agent (upstream)
nodes.role: Upstream NousResearch/hermes-agent NixOS module (services.hermes-agent) — provides the Podman-container agent
nodes.details: upstream services.hermes-agent; backend=podman, image ubuntu:24.04, --memory=4g --cpus=2; OpenRouter nemotron-3 free models; Telegram allowed users; host: hermes-claw

nodes.label: n8n-tailscale
nodes.role: n8n workflow automation wrapper (services.n8n-tailscale) — bind 127.0.0.1:5678, Tailscale Serve, declarative workflows
nodes.details: Imported & enabled on rpi5-full only

nodes.label: n8n-mcp-claude.nix
nodes.role: n8n MCP server for Claude Code (services.n8n-mcp-claude) — docs-only on rpi5, full workflow-management mode on rpi5-full
nodes.details: ns services.n8n-mcp-claude; not a daemon; oneshot per user merges mcpServers['n8n-mcp'] (npx n8n-mcp, stdio) into ~/.claude.json under flock; docs-only on rpi5 vs full mode (N8N_API_KEY+N8N_API_URL) on rpi5-full; duplicates home-assistant-mcp-claude RMW+flock logic.

nodes.label: n8n-skills.nix
nodes.role: Home-manager symlinks of czlonkowski/n8n-skills into ~/.claude/skills
nodes.details: ns services.n8n-skills; fetchFromGitHub pinned rev+hash; 7 skill dirs symlinked via home.file per user; no daemon, no port, no secret; enabled on rpi5 base (inherited by rpi5-full).

nodes.label: gatus-tailscale
nodes.role: Gatus declarative status monitoring (services.gatus-tailscale) — port 3001, Tailscale Serve, monitors local + sancta-claw endpoints
nodes.details: Imported & enabled on rpi5-full only

nodes.label: nixframe.nix
nodes.role: NixFrame digital photo frame (services.nixframe) — HDMI slideshow, weather + CalDAV calendar sidebar
nodes.details: ns services.nixframe; NO tailscale-serve of its own; photo intake via n8n nixframe-ui webhook; n8n (in nixframe group) writes photoDir + .trigger, watched by systemd.paths; CalDAV via caldav-credentials; ENABLED on rpi5-full.

nodes.label: services.backup-pull
nodes.role: Pull-backup service (services.backup-pull) — daily rsync from sancta-claw → tmpfs → restic encrypted repo
nodes.details: option ns services.backup-pull; rsync remote -> tmpfs staging -> restic; weekly check; Telegram failure alert; host: rpi5-full (remote=sancta-claw)

nodes.label: services.home-assistant-mcp-claude
nodes.role: Per-user oneshot merging hass-mcp (voska/hass-mcp via uvx) into ~/.claude.json
nodes.details: option ns services.home-assistant-mcp-claude.{enable,version,users,haUrl,tokenFile}; pins hass-mcp==0.4.1; injects HA_TOKEN at runtime; host: rpi5-full (users=[nixos])

nodes.label: services.unifi-mcp
nodes.role: UniFi Network MCP server (services.unifi-mcp) — AI-assisted network management, native python (no docker)
nodes.details: option ns services.unifi-mcp; builds unifi-network-mcp 0.6.4 + mcp 1.27.2 override; per-user oneshot merges 'unifi' into ~/.claude.json; injects UNIFI_PASSWORD at runtime; host: rpi5 (useDocker=false, stdio only) [corrected: stdio MCP, no listener; controller port default 443; 8765 only in disabled SSE mode]

nodes.label: copilot MCP config
nodes.role: Writes ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for root (context7 MCP via npx)
nodes.details: no options namespace, no enable flag — unconditional HM file for root; imported by rpi5 (=> rpi5-full)

nodes.label: qdrant.nix
nodes.role: Qdrant vector DB wrapper (services.qdrant-tailscale) — disabled (too heavy for RPi5)
nodes.details: Import commented out in rpi5-full; dead/disabled on all current hosts

nodes.label: dev-tools.nix
nodes.role: Optional dev-tools package set (editors, tmux, htop/btop, ripgrep/fd, sqlite, nodejs_22, gh, copilot, nix tooling, strace, nix-tree, playwright)
nodes.details: Defines option customModules.dev-tools.enable (mkEnableOption). config gated by mkIf. Installs github-copilot-cli from pkgs-unstable; sets PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH / PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_VALIDATE_HOST_REQUIREMENTS session vars. Also exported as flake nixosModules.dev-tools.

nodes.label: system/host.nix
nodes.role: sancta-choir-specific bare host config: hostName (mkForce), static eth0 networking, gateway, nameservers, openssh, root SSH key, vscode-server, firewall, timezone, locale
nodes.details: No options; pure config. Sets networking.hostName=sancta-choir (mkForce), eth0 116.203.223.113/32, gateway 172.31.1.1, nameservers 185.12.64.x, openssh PermitRootLogin=prohibit-password + PasswordAuthentication=false, root authorizedKeys, services.vscode-server, firewall TCP 22, tz Europe/Chisinau. Only imported by sancta-choir.

nodes.label: system/networking.nix
nodes.role: Hetzner Cloud static networking for sancta-choir (eth0 v4+v6, gateway, nameservers, MAC->eth0 udev rule)
nodes.details: No options; pure config. eth0 116.203.223.113/32 + ipv6 fe80::.../64, gateway 172.31.1.1, nameservers 8.8.8.8 + 185.12.64.x, usePredictableInterfaceNames=mkForce false, dhcpcd disabled, udev MAC binding 92:00:06:bb:96:03->eth0. Only imported by sancta-choir.

nodes.label: nix-ld.nix
nodes.role: Enables programs.nix-ld with a common library set so dynamically-linked non-Nix binaries run (VS Code extensions, external LSPs, uvx-spawned hass-mcp interpreter)
nodes.details: No custom option; turns on upstream programs.nix-ld.enable + libraries (glibc, stdenv.cc.cc.lib, zlib/zstd, openssl, curl, icu, libunwind, libuuid, util-linux, xorg X11 libs). Also exported as flake nixosModules.nix-ld.

nodes.label: ssh-hardened.nix
nodes.role: SSH hardening fragment for VPS hosts: PermitRootLogin=prohibit-password, PasswordAuthentication=false
nodes.details: No options; 2 settings only. Meant for hosts that should disable password auth + restrict root, leaving rpi5 base bootstrap-friendly. Imported by rpi5-full, sancta-claw, hermes-claw, zero-kuzea. NOT imported by sancta-choir (which open-codes the same two settings in host.nix).

nodes.label: open-webui-arm-fix.nix
nodes.role: aarch64 overlay: strip chromadb (onnxruntime crash) + disable polars/jemalloc checks
nodes.details: nixpkgs.overlay gated on isAarch64+Linux; removes chromadb from open-webui propagatedBuildInputs; disables checks on deepdiff/chromadb; NOT imported on any active host (commented in rpi5-full alongside open-webui).

nodes.label: users/root.nix
nodes.role: Home Manager config for root user: stateVersion 24.05, ~/.local/bin on PATH, git enabled, bash with a copilot shell alias to the pkgs-unstable github-copilot-cli
nodes.details: Requires pkgs-unstable specialArg. home-manager.users.root: home.sessionPath += ~/.local/bin, programs.git.enable, programs.bash with shellAlias copilot=${pkgs-unstable.github-copilot-cli}/bin/copilot (override VS Code's bundled shim). Imported by rpi5, rpi5-full (via rpi5), sancta-choir, sancta-claw, hermes-claw.

nodes.label: lib/secrets.nix
nodes.role: Helper functions for age.secrets declarations: secret (root-owned 0400) and ownedSecret (owner/group-scoped, e.g. openclaw/nullclaw)
nodes.details: Takes { self }, returns { secret = name: {file=...}; ownedSecret = owner: name: {file; owner; group}; }. file path = ${self}/secrets/<name>.age. Imported inline (import ../../lib/secrets.nix { inherit self; }) by sancta-choir, zero-kuzea, hermes-claw, rpi5-full, sancta-claw.

nodes.label: tailscale-auth-key
nodes.role: Tailscale auth key — used by every host's tailscale module
nodes.details: secret (root-owned). Declared on sancta-choir, sancta-claw, hermes-claw, zero-kuzea, rpi5

nodes.label: open-webui-secret-key
nodes.role: Open-WebUI session signing / JWT secret key
nodes.details: publicKeys=allKeys: root-sancta-choir, rpi5, sancta-choir

nodes.label: openrouter-api-key
nodes.role: OpenRouter API key — Open-WebUI (choir), ZDR proxy + auth (claw), n8n (rpi5-full)
nodes.details: publicKeys=allPlusBoth: root-sancta-choir, rpi5, sancta-choir, sancta-claw, hermes-claw

nodes.label: tavily-api-key
nodes.role: Tavily web-search API key for Open-WebUI
nodes.details: publicKeys=allKeys: root-sancta-choir, rpi5, sancta-choir

nodes.label: e2e-test-api-key
nodes.role: E2E test API key for Open-WebUI testing + Gatus monitor
nodes.details: publicKeys=allKeys: root-sancta-choir, rpi5, sancta-choir

nodes.label: kuzea-caldav-credentials
nodes.role: iCloud CalDAV creds for openclaw vdirsyncer setup
nodes.details: publicKeys=clawKeys: root-sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5

nodes.label: kuzea-github-token
nodes.role: GitHub PAT read by openclaw git-credential-agenix helper at runtime
nodes.details: publicKeys=clawKeys: root-sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5

nodes.label: kuzea-todoist-credentials
nodes.role: Todoist creds — injected into openclaw via EnvironmentFile
nodes.details: publicKeys=clawKeys: root-sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5

nodes.label: kuzea-airtable-credentials
nodes.role: Airtable creds — injected into openclaw via EnvironmentFile
nodes.details: publicKeys=clawKeys: root-sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5

nodes.label: kuzea-tavily-api-key
nodes.role: Tavily web-search key for Kuzea/OpenClaw (separate from open-webui tavily)
nodes.details: publicKeys=clawKeys: root-sancta-choir, sancta-claw, rpi5

narrative:
## What this repo is

A single NixOS flake driving a small personal fleet of **6 host configurations across 2 architectures**, all stitched together by Tailscale and agenix-encrypted secrets. The real-world roles split cleanly:

- **rpi5-full** (aarch64, the *only* truly active deploy target per CLAUDE.md) — the home server. It imports `rpi5/configuration.nix` wholesale and layers on the home-services stack: n8n workflow automation, Gatus monitoring, NixFrame photo frame, Home Assistant, and the backup-pull job. `rpi5` itself exists almost solely to build the SD image — it is labelled "minimal" but already pulls UniFi-MCP, n8n-MCP/skills, claude-shared and copilot, so the only genuine delta is rpi5-full's 6 extra service imports.
- **sancta-choir** (x86 Hetzner) — Open-WebUI AI chat gateway on the OpenRouter backend, fronted by Tailscale Serve HTTPS.
- **sancta-claw** (x86 CX33) — the dedicated OpenClaw/Kuzea Telegram agent. By far the most complex host (9 sub-files: the systemd unit, self-service watchers, Tailscale-serve front, backup-user + backup-acl, restore + smoke-test DR scripts). All model calls route through a local ZDR sidecar proxy on 127.0.0.1:5780.
- **hermes-claw** (x86 CX33) — Nous Research Hermes agent in a Podman container via an upstream flake module.
- **zero-kuzea** (x86 CX22) — minimal NullClaw Telegram bot (Anthropic Claude directly).

## How config flows

The layering is consistent and worth drawing as lanes: **flake inputs → host builders → host configs → service modules → system fragments → secrets → ops/CI**.

`flake.nix` declares ~15 inputs. Most `follows nixpkgs`; the lone exception is `nixos-raspberrypi`, which deliberately carries its own pinned nixpkgs (25.11) — the entire repo was bumped 25.05→25.11 *to align with it* and silence Home-Manager version warnings (#264). That alignment is load-bearing and fragile.

Each host is built by `nixpkgs.lib.nixosSystem` (or the raspberrypi builder for the two Pis), gets `pkgs-unstable` via specialArgs, and pulls in `agenix`, `home-manager`, `disko` (VPS only) and the hand-rolled inline `agenixModule` (just to add the agenix CLI). Every host imports `hosts/common.nix` (SSH keepalives, zram, fail2ban, flakes, `/bin/bash` symlink, core CLI). Service wrappers under `modules/services/` follow one strong pattern: **bind to 127.0.0.1, expose via a per-service `*-tailscale-serve` oneshot, pull secrets through `age.secrets`.** System fragments under `modules/system/` (nix-ld, ssh-hardened, dev-tools, the sancta-choir-only host/networking) and `modules/users/root.nix` round out the base.

## Secrets and trust

agenix runs **two trust models by design**: the core hosts (rpi5/rpi5-full, sancta-choir) use their SSH host key as the recipient (exposed to the documented fail-open-on-host-key-rotation trap), while the VPS hosts use a standalone `/root/.age/recovery.key` so a DR re-image decrypts on first boot. A subtle coupling: **zero-kuzea is not a distinct recipient at all** — it decrypts its two secrets only because it shares sancta-claw's recovery key, so one key compromise spans both boxes.

## Notable runtime topology

The fleet is mostly star-shaped around Tailscale, with two real cross-host edges: rpi5-full's `backup-pull` rsyncs `/var/lib/openclaw` **from** sancta-claw (rrsync-restricted `backup-user`), and sancta-claw's `restore.sh` pushes the inverse (ssh+restic restore from rpi5). NixFrame is unusual — it has **no listener of its own**; its entire upload ingress is the n8n `nixframe-ui` webhook, making n8n a hard runtime dependency of the photo-frame path. Gatus monitors n8n, Home Assistant and (across hosts) sancta-claw's OpenClaw healthz.

## The dominant theme: four agent runners, two Claude modules

The single biggest structural observation is **product fragmentation**: the same "Telegram + agent + OpenRouter/Anthropic" product is implemented three times across three dedicated VPSes (openclaw-host npm, nullclaw zig, hermes container) — plus a fully-built-but-**dead** fourth implementation, `modules/services/openclaw.nix` (~820 lines, zero importers, confirmed by grep). On top of that, Claude Code itself is provisioned two ways (legacy package-only `claude.nix` on the choir/claw VPSes vs the full `claude-shared` stack on the Pis), pulling the CC binary from two different inputs — a version-skew risk the repo's own header comments flag as a deprecation path.

simplifications.title: Delete the dead openclaw.nix module (~820 lines, zero importers)
simplifications.evidence: modules/services/openclaw.nix defines services.openclaw (claude -p file-inbox runner) but grep across hosts/ + flake.nix finds no `services.openclaw.enable` and no import of the file. The only live 'openclaw' is hosts/sancta-claw/openclaw-service.nix's inline `systemd.services.openclaw` (npm gateway) — a name collision. Its tailscaleServe block is even a self-described 'Phase 2 placeholder' that proxies nothing. CLAUDE.md still documents this dead module as the live `services.openclaw` AI partner.
simplifications.recommendation: Delete modules/services/openclaw.nix and remove the stale `services.openclaw` row + 'file-based inbox' description from CLAUDE.md's Services table. If any logic (nftables egress lockdown, restricted-sudo build wrapper) is worth keeping, lift it into openclaw-service.nix first.
simplifications.risk: Low — nothing imports it, so eval/build is unaffected. Only risk is losing the egress-lockdown reference design; mitigate by grepping it for reusable snippets before deletion.

verdict.title: Delete the dead openclaw.nix module (~820 lines, zero importers)
verdict.reason: The finding's central premise — "zero importers" / "grep across hosts/ + flake.nix finds no `services.openclaw.enable` and no import of the file" — is FALSE. `/Users/alexandru/nixos-config/tests/module-eval.nix` imports `../modules/services/openclaw.nix` three times (lines 413, 430, 450) and exercises `services.openclaw.enable` in three test cases: `openclaw-missing-claude-code` (shouldFail, asserts the null claude-code input fires), `openclaw-nix-store-secret-rejected` (shouldFail, asserts /nix/store secret rejection), and `openclaw-disabled` (shouldEval). Crucially, `module-eval` IS wired into CI: `/Users/alexandru/nixos-config/flake.nix` line 302 sets `checks.x86_64-linux.module-eval = import ./tests/module-eval.nix {...}`. tests/module-eval.nix:31-74 shows `evalConfig` imports each module path directly and `shouldEval` force-evaluates it with no tryEval guard, so deleting modules/services/openclaw.nix would make the import path nonexistent and break `nix flake check` (the repo's documented primary validation, run in CI per CLAUDE.md). The finding's risk line ("Low — nothing imports it, so eval/build is unaffected") is therefore factually wrong: a naive deletion DOES break eval. The secondary observations in the finding are accurate: there genuinely is a name collision (hosts/sancta-claw/openclaw-service.nix:346 declares an inline `systemd.services.openclaw` npm gateway, separate from the module's `services.openclaw`), the module's tailscale-serve-openclaw is a self-described "Phase 2 placeholder" (openclaw.nix:694), and CLAUDE.md's Services table does still document `services.openclaw` as the live "AI programming partner (Claude Code, file-based inbox)". But no host imports the module (only openclaw-zdr-proxy.nix is imported by sancta-claw), so the module is unused-in-production — yet not unreferenced. "Zero importers" is false and "~820 lines" is the only number that checks out (wc -l = 822).
verdict.correction: Do not delete modules/services/openclaw.nix in isolation. If the module is to be retired, the change must ALSO remove the three openclaw test cases (openclaw-missing-claude-code, openclaw-nix-store-secret-rejected, openclaw-disabled) from tests/module-eval.nix lines ~410-456, and the stale `services.openclaw` row from CLAUDE.md's Services table, then run `nix flake check` (the module-eval check) to prove eval is still green. Before deletion, lift any worth-keeping logic (per-UID nftables egress lockdown at openclaw.nix:812, restricted-sudo build wrapper, the /nix/store-secret assertion) — note the security assertions are currently the module's main remaining value, since they are tested even though no host enables the service.

simplifications.title: Consolidate the legacy claude.nix into claude-shared (retire the package-only fork)
simplifications.evidence: svc:claude (modules/services/claude.nix) is package-only and used by sancta-choir + sancta-claw; svc:claude-shared is a strict superset (same binary + skills/agents/commands) used by rpi5/rpi5-full. The two pull the CC binary from DIFFERENT inputs (input:claude-code vs claude-shared's bundled claude-code) — a version-skew risk. claude-shared.nix's own header says it 'replaces the in-repo claude*.nix modules'. CLAUDE.md documents the split as deliberate.
simplifications.recommendation: Add a headless/no-skills profile to claudeShared, migrate sancta-choir + sancta-claw onto it, then delete claude.nix and the `input:claude-code` specialArg wiring for those hosts. Removes one whole input-coupling path and the two-source version skew.
simplifications.risk: Med — claude-shared pulls home-manager wiring; on a small VPS that's extra eval/closure. Mitigate with an enable flag that installs only the package when skills aren't wanted.

verdict.title: Consolidate the legacy claude.nix into claude-shared (retire the package-only fork)
verdict.reason: Every load-bearing claim verifies against the repo. Wiring: hosts/sancta-choir/configuration.nix:44,51 and hosts/sancta-claw/configuration.nix:22,30 import modules/services/claude.nix and set customModules.claude.enable; hosts/rpi5/configuration.nix:33,43 imports modules/services/claude-shared.nix and sets customModules.claudeShared. rpi5-full does get claudeShared too (hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix:49 imports ../rpi5/configuration.nix), so the finding's 'rpi5/rpi5-full' is correct. Subset/superset: claude.nix:37-38 only adds claude-code.packages.<system>.default. The claude-shared HM module (~/.claude-shared/module/default.nix:269-287) installs the same upstream package PLUS skills/agents/commands symlinks and a user CLAUDE.md — a genuine functional superset. The header of claude-shared.nix:1-2 indeed states it 'replaces the in-repo claude{,-settings,-skills,-agents}.nix modules'. Version skew is real and confirmed in flake.lock: two distinct claude-code nodes — top-level claude-code rev 0b01bf3 (consumed by claude.nix via specialArgs in flake.nix:134,151) vs claude-shared's bundled claude-code_2 rev 304b96c (newer lastModified) consumed on rpi5/rpi5-full. So choir/claw and the Pis pull the CC binary from different inputs. Feasibility: both VPS hosts already wire home-manager (sancta-choir/configuration.nix:109, sancta-claw/configuration.nix:83), so no new HM plumbing is needed. CLAUDE.md documents the split as deliberate, matching the finding. Bonus: the top-level claude-code input's only other consumer is the dead modules/services/openclaw.nix (not imported by any host — confirmed), so retiring claude.nix really would remove a whole input-coupling path. Nothing here would break a host on eval; the consolidation is genuine, not meaningful divergence.
verdict.correction: Recommendation is directionally correct but understates one hard prerequisite: claude-shared's HM module is NOT a drop-in for a headless VPS as written. Under cfg.enable it unconditionally (a) runs a git-clone/pull of github.com/alexandru-savinov/claude-shared at activation (~/.claude-shared/module/default.nix:239-247), (b) symlinks skills/agents/commands into ~/.claude (lines 146-150,237), and (c) writes a user-level ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (line 288-290). zellijIntegration.enable=false (already set in the in-repo claude-shared.nix wrapper) does NOT suppress these. So the 'add a headless/no-skills profile' flag the finding proposes is a required precondition, not an optional nicety — without it, migrating choir/claw would start cloning the content repo and writing skill symlinks on each VPS at every rebuild. Correct sequence: (1) add e.g. installPackageOnly/contentless option to the claude-shared module that gates the clone activation + contentSymlinks + userClaudeMd off while keeping installPackage; (2) point that input's claude-code to the top-level rev (or accept the bundled one) to collapse the skew; (3) migrate choir+claw; (4) delete claude.nix and drop the top-level claude-code specialArg for those hosts. Note the top-level claude-code input also feeds the dead openclaw.nix, so it can only be fully dropped once that module (index-0 finding) is also deleted.

simplifications.title: Extract one mkTailscaleServe lib helper for the 6 copy-pasted serve oneshots
simplifications.evidence: The `tailscale serve status | grep -q https:PORT` idempotency guard + partOf/wantedBy/TimeoutStartSec=150 + wait-for-port loop + preStop `serve --https PORT off` is copy-pasted in n8n.nix:788, open-webui.nix:1133, unifi-mcp.nix:589, openclaw.nix:724, nullclaw.nix:390, qdrant.nix:195 (and gatus). qdrant.nix already factored a wait_for() helper; gatus uses a divergent grep pattern ('https?://.*:PORT') vs the others' 'https:PORT' — a latent inconsistency.
simplifications.recommendation: Add lib/mkTailscaleServe.nix taking {port, httpsPort, requires} that emits the standard oneshot, and reconcile the grep pattern to one form. Replaces ~6 near-identical units with one call each.
simplifications.risk: Low-Med — must preserve the #449 partOf-start-gap behavior and per-service port/preStop; verify with the existing open-webui-tavily / n8n-declarative nixosTests after refactor.

verdict.title: Extract one mkTailscaleServe lib helper for the 6 copy-pasted serve oneshots
verdict.reason: Verified by reading all cited files in /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/modules/services/. The `tailscale serve status | grep -q "https:PORT"` idempotency guard exists at EXACTLY the cited lines: n8n.nix:788, open-webui.nix:1133, unifi-mcp.nix:589, openclaw.nix:724, nullclaw.nix:390, qdrant.nix:195, plus gatus.nix:632 and home-assistant.nix:158. The surrounding oneshot shape (partOf=[main.service], wantedBy=[multi-user.target], serviceConfig Type=oneshot/RemainAfterExit=true/TimeoutStartSec=150, a tailscaled+port wait loop, and a preStop `serve --https PORT off`) is genuinely near-duplicated across the live service modules — real copy-paste, not meaningful divergence. The claimed grep-pattern inconsistency is CONFIRMED and even understated: gatus.nix:632 AND home-assistant.nix:158 both use the divergent `grep -qE "https?://.*:PORT( |$)"` while the other six use the literal `grep -q "https:PORT"` — a true latent inconsistency a helper would fix. qdrant.nix:178-189 already factored a `wait_for()` helper (as the finding states), and nullclaw.nix:374-385 has its own `wait_for()` too, proving the authors themselves saw the duplication. The referenced verification tests (tests/n8n-declarative.nix, tests/open-webui-tavily.nix) both exist. Removing the duplication does not break any host: the helper would emit the same units, and the listed nixosTests guard the behavior.
verdict.correction: Add lib/mkTailscaleServe.nix and reconcile the grep pattern to one form — sound. But scope corrections: (1) The helper must be parameterized over MORE than {port, httpsPort, requires}: the modules differ in health-check command (nc -z for n8n/open-webui/unifi-mcp/gatus/home-assistant/qdrant vs `curl -sf .../health` in nullclaw), upstream host (127.0.0.1 vs cfg.host in open-webui/qdrant), wait-loop style (inline while/until in 5 files vs wait_for() helper in qdrant+nullclaw), and preStop error handling (`|| true` vs explicit WARNING vs openclaw's custom message; nullclaw drops `--bg` in preStop). So pass {port, httpsPort, upstreamHost, healthCheck, requires} — it is not a trivial drop-in. (2) EXCLUDE openclaw.nix from the consolidation: it is dead code (finding index 0; `services.openclaw` is enabled on no host and CLAUDE.md documents it as legacy) and its oneshot is a self-described Phase-2 placeholder that proxies nothing. Delete it rather than fold it in; the live duplication is across ~6-7 modules (n8n, open-webui, unifi-mcp, nullclaw, qdrant, gatus, home-assistant), not the openclaw count. (3) home-assistant.nix is a real second consumer of the divergent grep pattern but is missing from the finding's nodeIds — add it.

simplifications.title: Factor the triplicated ~/.claude.json MCP-merge oneshot into lib/mkClaudeMcpOneshot.nix
simplifications.evidence: unifi-mcp.nix, home-assistant-mcp-claude.nix and n8n-mcp-claude.nix each implement the same flock + read-modify-write + atomic-mv merge of one MCP entry into ~/.claude.json (each grep-confirmed to call flock 2-3x); the files even cross-reference 'pattern mirrored from n8n-mcp-claude.nix'. ~80 lines duplicated 3x. copilot.nix is the odd one out (writes ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json, no enable option).
simplifications.recommendation: Add lib/mkClaudeMcpOneshot.nix taking {name, mcpConfig, users} and reduce the three modules to thin callers. Optionally fold n8n-skills (a pure HM symlink) under claude-shared content rather than services/.
simplifications.risk: Low — behavior-preserving; verify the merged ~/.claude.json still parses on rpi5-full after rebuild (no nixosTest exists for these, so check by hand).

verdict.title: Factor the triplicated ~/.claude.json MCP-merge oneshot into lib/mkClaudeMcpOneshot.nix
verdict.reason:
Verified by reading all four cited files. The triplication is real and the shared skeleton is functionally byte-identical across the three modules:

- modules/services/n8n-mcp-claude.nix (generateMcpConfigScript, lines 87-138, ~52 body lines)
- modules/services/home-assistant-mcp-claude.nix (lines 55-102, ~48 lines)
- modules/services/unifi-mcp.nix (lines 170-236, ~67 lines)

Each implements the same read-modify-write oneshot: `set -euo pipefail` + `USER_HOME=$(eval echo "~${user}")` + `CONFIG_FILE="$USER_HOME/.claude.json"`, then `exec 9>"$CONFIG_FILE.lock"` + `flock 9`, then the identical `if .mcpServers == null then .mcpServers = {}` jq init guard, a `<<'MCPEOF'` heredoc carrying `${builtins.toJSON mkMcpConfigBase}`, a runtime secret-injection step, and the identical atomic tail: `mktemp` -> `jq '.mcpServers["<name>"] = $x'` -> `chown` -> `chmod 600` -> `mv -f`. I diffed the init/lock/read blocks of n8n vs home-assistant directly — they differ only in comment wording. Each calls `${pkgs.util-linux}/bin/flock 9` exactly once; the finding's "flock 2-3x" grep count is corroborated (2/2/3, inflated by comment mentions, not a bug in the claim).

The cross-references the finding cites exist verbatim: home-assistant-mcp-claude.nix:18 "Pattern mirrored from modules/services/n8n-mcp-claude.nix" and unifi-mcp.nix:162 "mirroring the n8n / home-assistant siblings."

The only per-module variance maps cleanly onto helper parameters: (a) the MCP entry name (n8n-mcp / home-assistant / unifi), (b) the mkMcpConfigBase attrset, (c) the secret-injection step — n8n's nullable apiKeyFile (optionalString), HA's nullable tokenFile (optionalString), unifi's mandatory passwordFile with a useDocker/non-docker arg-vs-env branch. So `{name, mcpConfig, users}` plus an injection hook captures all three; the finding's signature is essentially right but understated on the secret hook.

copilot.nix (lines 1-16) is confirmed the odd one out: it writes ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json via home-manager.users.root with no enable option and no flock/RMW — correctly excluded.

Safety: the three modules are enabled only on rpi5/rpi5-full (unifi-mcp + n8n-mcp on rpi5; n8n-mcp + home-assistant-mcp on rpi5-full), all aarch64 hosts; no VPS uses them, so a behavior-preserving extraction can't break a remote host. lib/ already exists at repo root (currently only lib/secrets.nix), so adding lib/mkClaudeMcpOneshot.nix fits the layout.
verdict.correction: Recommendation is sound but tighten the helper signature: `lib/mkClaudeMcpOneshot.nix` taking `{name, mcpConfigBase, users, injectSecret ? null}` where injectSecret is a shell snippet (or function of the config var name) that the body splices in before the atomic write — this is needed to cover unifi's mandatory-password + docker/non-docker branch and the two siblings' nullable-secret optionalString guards, which a bare `{name, mcpConfig, users}` does not express. Preserve unifi's documented load-bearing 4-space heredoc indentation inside the helper and re-run `nix fmt` after. Drop the "fold n8n-skills under claude-shared" clause from this finding's scope — n8n-skills.nix is a separate HM-symlink module (imported on rpi5) unrelated to the MCP-merge duplication; bundling it muddies an otherwise clean, behavior-preserving refactor.

simplifications.title: Create hosts/vps-common.nix + disk-cx-vps.nix to kill the 3-way VPS duplication
simplifications.evidence: hosts/{sancta-claw,hermes-claw,zero-kuzea}/disk-config.nix are byte-identical except a header comment (confirmed by diff). The three VPS hosts also share an identical block: useDHCP, usePredictableInterfaceNames=mkForce false, nameservers [8.8.8.8 185.12.64.1 185.12.64.2], firewall [22], Europe/Chisinau tz, age.identityPaths /root/.age/recovery.key, 4GB swapfile (#451/#252), and the same two root authorizedKeys (also pasted into sancta-choir).
simplifications.recommendation: Add hosts/system/disk-cx-vps.nix (shared disko layout) and hosts/vps-common.nix (DHCP/nameservers/tz/recovery-key/authorizedKeys/swap). Import from the three VPS hosts; pull the shared authorizedKeys list into one place referenced by all four.
simplifications.risk: Med — disko on the wrong device is destructive; keep /dev/sda parameterizable and re-run `nix eval` per host + the module-eval check. Recovery-key path is load-bearing for DR.

verdict.title: Create hosts/vps-common.nix + disk-cx-vps.nix to kill the 3-way VPS duplication
verdict.reason:
Verified by reading all cited files. The finding has two claims; one holds, the central one does not.

DISK-CONFIG (partly holds): The disko *payload* is functionally identical across all three — same `/dev/sda`, gpt, EF02 1M boot / 256M vfat ESP / 100% ext4 root — in hosts/sancta-claw/disk-config.nix (L15-54), hosts/hermes-claw/disk-config.nix, hosts/zero-kuzea/disk-config.nix (L3-42). But "byte-identical except a header comment (confirmed by diff)" is only true for sancta-claw vs hermes-claw (diff = 1 comment line). zero-kuzea differs by a whole comment block (2 lines vs 14 lines of sgdisk instructions), not "a header comment". The shared disko layout is real and worth factoring, so disk-cx-vps.nix is justified.

SHARED SYSTEM BLOCK (does NOT hold — the load-bearing claim): The finding asserts "the three VPS hosts also share an identical block" of useDHCP + usePredictableInterfaceNames=mkForce false + nameservers [8.8.8.8 185.12.64.1 185.12.64.2] + firewall [22] + Europe/Chisinau tz + recovery-key + 4GB swapfile + authorizedKeys. This is overstated:
 - Networking (useDHCP, usePredictableInterfaceNames, nameservers, firewall [22]) and tz/locale (Europe/Chisinau) exist in sancta-claw/configuration.nix (L36-50) and hermes-claw/configuration.nix (L22-36) — but NOT in zero-kuzea. grep for useDHCP|nameservers|usePredictableInterfaceNames|firewall|timeZone across hosts/zero-kuzea/ returns NONE FOUND; zero-kuzea only sets networking.hostName (L22) and relies on NixOS defaults. common.nix and zero-kuzea/hardware-configuration.nix carry no networking. So this block is shared by only 2 of 3 hosts, not 3.
 - "4GB swapfile" is wrong: swap sizes are 12288 (sancta-claw L92), 4096 (hermes-claw L64), 2048 (zero-kuzea L29) — three different values. Only hermes-claw is 4GB; swap is per-host, not a shareable constant.
 - Genuinely shared across all three: age.identityPaths = /root/.age/recovery.key, and the two root authorizedKeys (also present verbatim in sancta-choir/configuration.nix L126-127).

Because the central "identical block across the three VPS hosts" is false (networking/tz cover only 2 hosts; swap differs across all 3), the proposed single vps-common.nix imported by all three would either force DHCP/nameservers/tz onto zero-kuzea (a behavior change, not a refactor) or carry a wrong swap value. The duplication that actually exists is narrow: the disko layout (3 hosts) + recovery-key path + authorizedKeys (3-4 hosts).
verdict.correction: Split into two narrower, honest moves: (1) Add hosts/system/disk-cx-vps.nix with the shared disko layout (genuinely identical payload in all three -claw/-kuzea hosts); keep the device path parameterizable (all three hardcode /dev/sda). (2) Hoist only the two truly-shared bits into a small shared file: the two root authorizedKeys (shared by sancta-claw, hermes-claw, zero-kuzea AND sancta-choir) and age.identityPaths=/root/.age/recovery.key. Do NOT fold useDHCP/nameservers/firewall/tz into a "vps-common" imported by all three — zero-kuzea deliberately omits all of it; at most factor a networking snippet shared by sancta-claw+hermes-claw only. Do NOT put swap in the shared file — the three sizes are 12G/4G/2G, intentionally per-host.

simplifications.title: De-duplicate sancta-choir SSH hardening and networking (host.nix vs networking.nix vs ssh-hardened.nix)
simplifications.evidence: host.nix:32-33 open-codes PermitRootLogin=prohibit-password + PasswordAuthentication=false — the exact pair in ssh-hardened.nix:8-9 which the other 4 VPS hosts import; sancta-choir is the only VPS NOT importing ssh-hardened. Separately host.nix and networking.nix BOTH declare eth0 116.203.223.113, gateway 172.31.1.1, nameservers 185.12.64.x for the same host (grep-confirmed) — double-maintained.
simplifications.recommendation: Make sancta-choir import modules/system/ssh-hardened.nix and drop the inline SSH block; collapse the networking half of the mis-named host.nix into networking.nix (or inline both into hosts/sancta-choir/configuration.nix). Removes a 3-way SSH split and a duplicated IP/gateway/DNS declaration.
simplifications.risk: Med — networking changes on a remote VPS can lock you out; verify eth0/gateway/DNS resolve identically via `nix eval` of the final networking attrs and prefer `nixos-rebuild boot` for the switch.

verdict.title: De-duplicate sancta-choir SSH hardening and networking (host.nix vs networking.nix vs ssh-hardened.nix)
verdict.reason:
Verified by reading the files and by `nix eval` of the live sancta-choir config (eval succeeds, cross-platform-pure).

SSH claim — CONFIRMED. /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/modules/system/host.nix lines 32-33 open-code `PermitRootLogin = "prohibit-password"` and `PasswordAuthentication = false`, the exact pair in /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/modules/system/ssh-hardened.nix lines 8-9. grep confirms sancta-choir is the only x86 VPS that does NOT import ssh-hardened: sancta-claw (configuration.nix:25), hermes-claw (:15), zero-kuzea (:18) all import it (rpi5-full:64 too). `nix eval` of config.services.openssh.settings.PermitRootLogin returns "prohibit-password", proving the inline block is what is live.

Networking duplication — CONFIRMED and stronger than the finding states. host.nix and networking.nix BOTH set networking.interfaces.eth0.ipv4.addresses, networking.defaultGateway, networking.nameservers, networking.useDHCP for the same host, and both are imported only by sancta-choir (configuration.nix:40-41 — no other importer per grep). Because these are list/mergeable options at equal priority they concatenate rather than conflict, so the config still evaluates — but eval proves the redundancy leaks real duplicates: config.networking.interfaces.eth0.ipv4.addresses = [116.203.223.113/32, 116.203.223.113/32] (the SAME address twice) and config.networking.nameservers = ["8.8.8.8","185.12.64.1","185.12.64.2","185.12.64.1","185.12.64.2"] (185.12.64.1/.2 listed twice). There is also genuine drift: host.nix nameservers omit 8.8.8.8 while networking.nix includes it — exactly the double-maintenance hazard the finding warns about. So this is not benign overlap; it is meaningful, observable redundancy that one consolidated source would fix.

Severity med is appropriate; the recommendation (import ssh-hardened, drop the inline SSH block, collapse the two networking declarations into one) is correct and would remove the duplicate runtime entries with no behavior loss (gateway/address are identical; nameserver union just needs the intended set chosen).
verdict.correction: Recommendation is sound. Two precision corrections: (1) The files are modules/system/host.nix and modules/system/networking.nix (shared-module location), NOT hosts/sancta-choir/ files as the finding's phrasing implies — but both are imported ONLY by sancta-choir, so they are effectively host-specific and the consolidation argument holds. The mis-named host.nix is the root issue: it is a sancta-choir-only networking+SSH+misc module masquerading as a generic system module. (2) When collapsing, explicitly pick ONE nameserver list — host.nix drops 8.8.8.8, networking.nix keeps it; today both leak so you get a 5-entry list with duplicates. Decide whether 8.8.8.8 is wanted, then declare nameservers once.

simplifications.title: Wire or delete the three orphaned nixosTests; they rot silently
simplifications.evidence: flake.nix checks.x86_64-linux (lines 294-310) wires ONLY module-eval and openclaw-zdr-proxy. tests/n8n-declarative.nix, open-webui-tavily.nix and qdrant-integration.nix are referenced by no workflow and use the legacy `{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> {} }` channel pattern instead of the flake's pinned pkgs — runnable only by hand, never in CI.
simplifications.recommendation: Add n8n-declarative + open-webui-tavily to checks.x86_64-linux (they assert real env wiring on rpi5-full/sancta-choir paths) and convert them to the flake's pinned pkgs; drop qdrant-integration.nix since Qdrant runs nowhere (see next finding).
simplifications.risk: Low — adding checks only lengthens CI; converting away from <nixpkgs> is mechanical. Watch CI runtime since these boot VMs.

verdict.title: Wire or delete the three orphaned nixosTests; they rot silently
verdict.reason: Every claim verified against the real repo. flake.nix lines 294-310 (checks.x86_64-linux) wire ONLY module-eval (line 302) and openclaw-zdr-proxy (line 309); the three tests are absent. All three files begin with the legacy channel pattern `{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> { } }:` — tests/n8n-declarative.nix:1, tests/open-webui-tavily.nix:1, tests/qdrant-integration.nix:13 — vs the flake's pinned `nixpkgsFor.x86_64-linux` used by the wired checks. No workflow references them: grep across .github/workflows/*.yml finds zero hits; check.yml line 46 runs only `nix flake check --all-systems` (so only the two wired checks execute) and python-tests.yml line 47 runs pytest, not nixosTests. The recommendation's targeting is sound: n8n-declarative exercises modules/services/n8n.nix's n8n-tailscale, enabled on hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix; open-webui-tavily exercises open-webui-tailscale, enabled on both sancta-choir and rpi5-full; qdrant-integration tests qdrant which runs nowhere (hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix:228-229 has it commented out), making the suggested deletion consistent with finding #7. These are genuinely un-run, rotting test assets — real dead-loop, not meaningful divergence.
verdict.correction: Recommendation is essentially correct; two precision notes. (1) checks.x86_64-linux is x86-only by deliberate design (flake.nix comment lines 291-293: CI runs --all-systems on x86 runners without aarch64 builders), so adding these VM-boot tests there is the right home, but they WILL boot full VMs and noticeably increase CI wall-clock — the risk field already flags this; budget for it rather than calling it merely 'lengthens CI'. (2) Both n8n-declarative and open-webui-tavily already disable tailscaleServe/oidc in-test, so converting them to the flake's pinned pkgs (replace the `{ pkgs ? import <nixpkgs> {} }` head with `pkgs.testers.nixosTest (import ./tests/<f>.nix { inherit pkgs; })` wiring like openclaw-zdr-proxy at flake.nix:309) is indeed mechanical. Drop qdrant-integration.nix per finding #7.

simplifications.title: Remove the dormant RAG-on-ARM stack (qdrant + open-webui-arm-fix)
simplifications.evidence: svc:qdrant / pkg:qdrant / qdrant-integration test and sys:open-webui-arm-fix run on NO active host — both commented out in rpi5-full as too heavy for RPi5, and open-webui (their only consumer) runs only on x86 sancta-choir which needs neither. open-webui talks to qdrant only when vectorDb.type=qdrant, which is not set anywhere.
simplifications.recommendation: Delete modules/services/qdrant.nix, modules/system/open-webui-arm-fix.nix, tests/qdrant-integration.nix and the commented-out rpi5-full import/Gatus-endpoint blocks. If RAG-on-ARM is a real future goal, keep them on a documented branch instead of dead in main.
simplifications.risk: Low — nothing enables them today. Only risk is losing the ARM overlay reference if Open-WebUI ever moves to a Pi; note that intent in a commit message.

verdict.title: Remove the dormant RAG-on-ARM stack (qdrant + open-webui-arm-fix)
verdict.reason:
The core premise is verified true. (1) modules/services/qdrant.nix, modules/system/open-webui-arm-fix.nix, and tests/qdrant-integration.nix all exist. (2) In hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix the qdrant import (line 54), the open-webui-arm-fix import (line 52), the services.qdrant-tailscale block (lines 229-230) and the Gatus qdrant endpoint (line 272) are ALL commented out. (3) open-webui-arm-fix.nix is imported by NO active host (only the commented rpi5-full reference). (4) The only live open-webui consumer is sancta-choir (x86_64-linux, hosts/sancta-choir/configuration.nix:71-97), which sets memory/tavily/zdr but NO vectorDb.type, so it defaults to "chromadb" (open-webui.nix:162) and never touches qdrant. No host anywhere sets vectorDb.type="qdrant" or services.qdrant. So the stack is genuinely dormant — nothing enables it today.

HOWEVER, the recommendation as written ("Delete modules/services/qdrant.nix") is imprecise and its "Low risk / eval unaffected" rating is UNDERSTATED. tests/module-eval.nix:93-119 imports ../modules/services/qdrant.nix in three live test cases (qdrant-minimal, qdrant-on-disk, qdrant-disabled), and module-eval IS a live CI check wired into flake.nix checks.x86_64-linux (lines 301-303, the same one finding #6 confirms is the only wired check besides openclaw-zdr-proxy). Deleting qdrant.nix without also removing those three cases from module-eval.nix would break `nix flake check` and CI. The finding's evidence section never mentions this coupling and the nodeIds omit test:module-eval. open-webui-arm-fix.nix has zero live importers and zero CI coupling, so deleting it is genuinely Low risk. open-webui.nix also still carries the vectorDb option machinery (lines 159-225, 540-664, 735-736) which would become dead config if qdrant is dropped — worth noting but not strictly broken.
verdict.correction: Keep the simplification but make it precise: (a) Delete modules/system/open-webui-arm-fix.nix and tests/qdrant-integration.nix (both orphaned, safe). (b) When deleting modules/services/qdrant.nix, ALSO remove the three qdrant cases (qdrant-minimal/qdrant-on-disk/qdrant-disabled, lines 93-119) from tests/module-eval.nix in the same change, or `nix flake check`/CI will break. (c) Remove the commented qdrant/arm-fix import and Gatus-endpoint lines in hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix (lines 52, 54, 229-230, 272). (d) Optionally prune the now-dead vectorDb=qdrant branches in modules/services/open-webui.nix and the qdrant-tailscale row in CLAUDE.md's Services table.

verdict.title: Archive stale root-level migration .md notes and the lone completed/ dir
verdict.reason: Every factual claim checks out against the real repo. Root carries the three named one-off writeups: /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/MIGRATION-RESUME.md (Apr 18), openrouter-vkeys-c-migration.md (May 23), plan-open-webui-sancta-choir.md (Apr 18) — Apr-May dates confirmed. /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/docs/ holds kuzea-plan.md (exactly 105635 bytes ~= the claimed 105KB), nixframe-plan.md, OPENCLAW-MICROVM-PLAN.md, and TELEGRAM-OPENCLAW-SETUP.md. The two competing archive conventions are real: /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/completed/plan-home-assistant.md exists at root alongside /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/docs/plans/completed/ (which contains 20260508-hermes-claw-upstream-module-container-mode.md and logical-finding-flurry.md). The 'no live references' claim holds for functional dependencies: grep across scripts/, .github/, and flake.nix returns nothing, and no .nix file imports any of these markdown docs. The only grep hits for the migration files are docs/architecture-map.html and docs/architecture-graph.json, which are NOT git-tracked (this review's own untracked analysis artifacts, timestamped today) so they don't count as live references. README.md, SECRETS-ROTATION.md, and CLAUDE.md are correctly identified as the live docs to keep. This is a genuine, behavior-neutral housekeeping move.
verdict.correction: The recommendation is sound but should add one step: completed/plan-home-assistant.md is referenced by bare filename in three live source comments — secrets/secrets.nix:127, hosts/rpi5-full/configuration.nix:327, and modules/services/home-assistant-mcp-claude.nix:22 (each 'see plan-home-assistant.md Task 5/6'). Moving that file into docs/plans/completed/ won't break eval/build (comments only), but those three pointers should be updated to the new path (or left as bare-filename references, which remain findable). Everything else in the recommendation (move the 3 root migration files + historical docs/ plans into docs/plans/completed/, fold root completed/ into it, keep README/SECRETS-ROTATION/CLAUDE.md live) is accurate.

simplifications.title: Collapse duplicated-plaintext secrets (Tavily, CalDAV, Telegram, hermes-env)
simplifications.evidence: Per secrets map: tavily-api-key vs kuzea-tavily-api-key (same vendor, two ciphertexts), caldav-credentials vs kuzea-caldav-credentials, and three Telegram bot tokens. hermes-env.age embeds the same OPENROUTER_API_KEY plaintext that already lives in openrouter-api-key.age (secrets.nix:148-150), so rotating one silently desyncs the other. hermes-env also has the known recipient drift (5 on-disk vs 3 declared) tracked by check-secret-recipients.sh.
simplifications.recommendation: Where the plaintext is genuinely identical, prefer a single .age recipient-broadened to all consuming hosts over per-host duplicates; for hermes-env, build the env file from the existing openrouter-api-key + telegram token at activation rather than re-encrypting the combined plaintext. Then clear the KNOWN_PENDING_DRIFT entry.
simplifications.risk: Med — broadening a secret's recipients widens blast radius; rotating shared keys touches multiple hosts at once. Do per-secret, re-run check-secret-recipients.sh and a deploy after each.

verdict.title: Collapse duplicated-plaintext secrets (Tavily, CalDAV, Telegram, hermes-env)
verdict.reason: Partially verified against /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/secrets/secrets.nix and scripts/check-secret-recipients.sh. The hermes-env half of the finding is solid and actionable. secrets.nix:147-151 explicitly states hermes-env.age "Contains OPENROUTER_API_KEY + TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN (same plaintext as the per-value secrets above, combined into one env file)" used only as `environmentFiles` for the upstream module in hosts/hermes-claw/hermes-service.nix:62. Critically, openrouter-api-key.age is declared `allPlusBoth` (secrets.nix:73), which INCLUDES hermes-claw, and zero-kuzea-telegram-bot-token.age is `clawKeys ++ [hermes-claw]` (secrets.nix:142, comment: "also re-keyed for hermes-claw so the Hermes Agent can decrypt the same plaintext"). So BOTH source plaintexts are already decryptable on hermes-claw — meaning the recommendation to build the env file from them at activation instead of re-encrypting a combined blob is genuinely feasible, and the rotate-one-desyncs-the-other risk is real. The recipient-drift claim is confirmed exactly: `grep -a -c '^-> '` gives hermes-env.age=5 stanzas vs declared `users ++ [rpi5 hermes-claw]`=3 (secrets.nix:151), matching the KNOWN_PENDING_DRIFT entry in check-secret-recipients.sh:53-55 (issue #448). HOWEVER the tavily/caldav/telegram half is imprecise and conflates deliberate least-privilege separation with redundancy: kuzea-tavily-api-key.age / kuzea-caldav-credentials.age are scoped to `clawKeys` (sancta-claw + owner only) while tavily-api-key.age / caldav-credentials.age are `allKeys` (rpi5 + sancta-choir) — these are intentionally different recipient sets for different hosts (sancta-claw's openclaw-service vs sancta-choir's open-webui / rpi5's nixframe), and the plaintexts may be genuinely different vendor keys/accounts, which the finding never proves. The "three Telegram bot tokens" (telegram-bot-token=n8n tender-monitor, zero-kuzea-telegram-bot-token=NullClaw bot, backup-telegram-env=backup-alert env with TOKEN+CHAT_ID) serve three distinct bots and are almost certainly NOT the same plaintext, so they are not consolidation candidates at all — listing them weakens the finding. Severity "low" and risk "Med" are correctly rated, and the recommendation is appropriately hedged ("Where the plaintext is genuinely identical").
verdict.correction: Split this into two findings. STRONG/keep: For hermes-env, stop maintaining a combined re-encrypted blob — both its values are already independently decryptable on hermes-claw (openrouter-api-key.age via allPlusBoth, zero-kuzea-telegram-bot-token.age via the explicit +hermes-claw re-key). Generate the KEY=VALUE env file at activation from those two existing secrets (e.g. a small activation script / systemd ExecStartPre that cats them into a 0400 file owned by hermes), delete hermes-env.age, and clear the KNOWN_PENDING_DRIFT entry in check-secret-recipients.sh. WEAK/drop or downgrade: Do NOT present tavily/kuzea-tavily, caldav/kuzea-caldav, or the three Telegram tokens as duplicated plaintext. The kuzea-* variants are a deliberate least-privilege split (clawKeys vs allKeys) for sancta-claw; the three Telegram tokens are different bots. Before touching any of these, first decrypt and prove the plaintexts are byte-identical AND that broadening recipients does not violate the intended host isolation — most likely they should stay separate.

simplifications.title: Add mkRpiHost/mkX86Host helpers and fix zero-kuzea's inline unstable import
simplifications.evidence: rpi5 and rpi5-full are near-identical flake wirings differing only in the configuration.nix path; the four x86 hosts share the nixosSystem + pkgs-unstable-x86 + agenix-stack shape. zero-kuzea re-imports nixpkgs-unstable inline `import nixpkgs-unstable { system=...; }` with NO config.allowUnfree (vs the shared pkgs-unstable-x86 the other three reuse) — a second instantiation and a latent allowUnfree breakage. agenixModule is a hand-rolled inline module duplicated into all 6 hosts.
simplifications.recommendation: Add mkRpiHost and mkX86Host helpers in flake.nix; change zero-kuzea to `inherit pkgs-unstable;`; fold agenixModule + agenix.nixosModules.default into hosts/common.nix so they aren't repeated per host.
simplifications.risk: Low — pure flake refactor caught by `nix flake check`/eval. zero-kuzea fix removes an eval-cost duplicate with no behavior change today.

verdict.title: Add mkRpiHost/mkX86Host helpers and fix zero-kuzea's inline unstable import
verdict.reason: Verified against /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/flake.nix and the host configs. (1) rpi5 (lines 198-211) and rpi5-full (219-232) are byte-identical wirings except the configuration.nix path — mkRpiHost is genuinely warranted. (2) zero-kuzea (line 183) inlines `import nixpkgs-unstable { system = "x86_64-linux"; }` with NO config.allowUnfree, while sancta-choir/sancta-claw/hermes-claw (lines 133/149/166) all reuse the shared `pkgs-unstable-x86` binding (lines 82-85) which sets `config.allowUnfree = true`. This is a real second nixpkgs-unstable instantiation (eval-cost duplicate) plus a latent allowUnfree gap. (3) agenixModule (defined once at line 92) is appended to all 6 host module lists (grep: 6 occurrences) and could fold into hosts/common.nix, which all 6 hosts import — directly for 5, transitively for rpi5-full via ../rpi5/configuration.nix. The structural redundancy genuinely exists and consolidating it would not break any host (eval-only refactor, no behavior change today).
verdict.correction: The zero-kuzea fix and the agenixModule fold-into-common.nix are both clean, high-value, and correct as written. The mkRpiHost helper is also justified (rpi5/rpi5-full are identical bar one path). However, mkX86Host is imprecise: the four x86 hosts do NOT share a uniform shape — sancta-choir has no disko, adds vscode-server + owui-openrouter-stats specialArg; sancta-claw adds disko + kuzea-ws specialArg, no vscode-server; hermes-claw adds disko + hermes-agent.nixosModules.default; zero-kuzea has disko but NO home-manager and NO claude-code. A single mkX86Host would need significant parameterization (extra modules list + extra specialArgs), so its payoff is modest. Recommend prioritizing the two concrete fixes (zero-kuzea `inherit pkgs-unstable;` and folding agenixModule + agenix.nixosModules.default into common.nix) and treating mkX86Host as optional, with the helper accepting `extraModules`/`extraSpecialArgs` to absorb the per-host divergence.

simplifications.title: Align bootstrap.sh throttle + stale host help, and .gitignore .ralphex/
simplifications.evidence: deploy.sh/install.sh apply --max-jobs 1 --cores 1 (the #252 OOM fix) but bootstrap.sh's apply_config does NOT — the exact unthrottled small-VPS rebuild the throttle was meant to prevent. bootstrap.sh + install.sh help text still list only 'sancta-choir, rpi5' though the repo now has 5+ hosts. .ralphex/ (~1.9MB of progress/, worktrees/, scratch PR bodies, host-key .pub files) is agent scratch checked into the repo root. .specify/ appears unused/vestigial.
simplifications.recommendation: Add --max-jobs 1 --cores 1 to bootstrap.sh's rebuild; refresh the host lists in both help texts; add .ralphex/ to .gitignore (and remove from tracking); confirm .specify/ is unused and delete it.
simplifications.risk: Low — script/ops hygiene. The throttle change only makes bootstrap safer; verify it still completes on a real bootstrap run.

verdict.title: Align bootstrap.sh throttle + stale host help, and .gitignore .ralphex/
verdict.reason:
This finding bundles four sub-claims; verified against the real repo at /Users/alexandru/nixos-config, two hold but two do not, and the two flagship items (.ralphex/ and .specify/) are wrong.

1) bootstrap.sh missing throttle — TRUE. scripts/bootstrap.sh apply_config() runs `nixos-rebuild build` (line 330) and `switch` (line 344) with NO `--max-jobs 1 --cores 1`. scripts/deploy.sh (lines 121, 152) and scripts/install.sh (lines 100, 126) both carry the throttle. CLAUDE.md documents the #252 OOM context. Genuine inconsistency. (Minor caveat: bootstrap.sh is banner-scoped to fresh RPi5/aarch64 infect, but it does accept any host arg, so the throttle is still warranted.)

2) Stale host help text — TRUE. scripts/bootstrap.sh:41 lists "sancta-choir, rpi5"; scripts/install.sh:27-28 lists only sancta-choir + rpi5. flake.nix:128-238 defines 6 nixosConfigurations (sancta-choir, sancta-claw, hermes-claw, zero-kuzea, rpi5, rpi5-full). Help text is stale.

3) ".gitignore .ralphex/ (and remove from tracking)" — FALSE / already done. .ralphex/ is ALREADY the last entry in /Users/alexandru/nixos-config/.gitignore ("# ralphex scratch state ..." then ".ralphex/"). `git ls-files .ralphex/` returns 0 files and `git log --all -- .ralphex/` is empty: it was never tracked. There is even a nested .ralphex/.gitignore. The evidence's premise that .ralphex/ is "agent scratch checked into the repo root" is factually false, and the recommended action is a no-op.

4) ".specify/ appears unused/vestigial ... confirm unused and delete it" — WRONG and risky. .specify/ has 12 git-tracked files (scripts + templates + memory/constitution.md). All 8 .github/prompts/speckit.*.prompt.md prompts invoke `.specify/scripts/bash/check-prerequisites.sh` (Spec-Kit / speckit integration). It is wired and live, not vestigial; deleting it would break that workflow.

Net: the two valid parts are real but low-severity script hygiene; the headline action (.ralphex/ gitignore) is already complete and the .specify/ delete recommendation is incorrect/destructive. The finding as written does not genuinely hold.
verdict.correction: Drop the .ralphex/ item entirely — it is already in .gitignore and was never tracked (verified via git ls-files / git log). Do NOT delete .specify/ — it is the active Spec-Kit integration referenced by all 8 .github/prompts/speckit.*.prompt.md prompts via .specify/scripts/bash/check-prerequisites.sh. Keep only the two valid, low-severity fixes: (a) add `--max-jobs 1 --cores 1` to scripts/bootstrap.sh apply_config()'s `nixos-rebuild build` (line 330) and `switch` (line 344) to match deploy.sh/install.sh and the #252 OOM throttle; (b) refresh the host lists in scripts/bootstrap.sh:41 and scripts/install.sh:27-28 to reflect the 6 current host configs.

accuracy.headline: The architecture map is broadly trustworthy: structurally correct on inputs, hosts, and wiring, with a handful of well-localized node-field errors, three fabricated secret edges, and five orphaned hardware-config nodes that should be fixed before relying on it.
